channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: You’re too big for REA chairmanship, Fayose’s brother tells ex-governor
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Isaac Fayose tells his elder brother, former Governor Ayo Fayose, to reject his Rural Electrification Agency (REA) chairmanship, calling it beneath his sta
Read More: https://punchng.com/youre-too-big-for-rea-chairmanship-fayoses-brother-tells-ex-governor/

item.title: Ombudsman's half-sister makes debut at Wolverhampton
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The Sky Sports Racing cameras head to the Midlands today, with evening racing from Wolverhampton on the agenda.

item.title: Prenga can beat AJ 'like Ruiz did', says sparring partner
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Kristian Prenga has been backed to upset Anthony Joshua "like [Andy] Ruiz did" by sparring partner Brandon Moore ahead of their fight on Saturday in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: World Cup final: What Messi told me after Spain beat Argentina – Lamine Yamal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Spain winger, Lamine Yamal, has revealed what Argentina captain, Lionel Messi told him after both teams’ World Cup final on Sunday. Yamal and Messi shared a moving hug after Spain defeated Argentina to win the World Cup in New Jersey. Ferran Torres’ extra-time goal gave the La Roja

item.title: Nigeria: Captured 'Ansaru Leaders' Make U-Turn, Plead Guilty to 32 Terrorism Charges
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The two Ansaru leaders pleaded guilty to all 32 charges, including terrorism, terrorism financing, kidnapping for ransom, illegal mining, funding terrorism, fabricating improvised explosive devices and related offences.

item.title: Gas supply rises 24.4% to 5. 1 metric tonnes daily — NMDPRA
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: •As retail price reduces to N1,400/Kg By Mariam Eko The supply of Liquefied Petroleum Gas, LPG, also known as cooking gas increased on a month-on-month, MoM basis by 24 percent to 5.1 metric tonnes per day in June 2026 from 4.1 metric tonnes per day in May 2026. This was disclosed in the data suppli

item.title: ‘We know ourselves well’ – Kwankwaso opens up on ties with Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Vice-presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Rabiu Kwankwaso, has spoken about his relationship with former Borno State Governor, Ali Modu Sheriff, saying they have known one each other since the aborted Third Republic. Kwankwaso made the remarks while responding to comments

item.title: Gov Alia appoints Ngbajime as Benue Assembly Service Commission member
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Benue State Governor, Rev Fr Hyacinth Alia, has approved the appointment of Dr. Anya Godwin Ngbajime, as a member of the Benue State House of Assembly Service Commission, subject to confirmation by the State House of Assembly.‎ ‎The appointment follows the vacancy created by the removal of the forme

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Tuesday morning
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers on Tuesday morning. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Tuesday morning appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Yamal leads Ballon d’Or race after inspiring Spain’s World Cup triumph
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Spain's World Cup victory has propelled teenage sensation Lamine Yamal to the top of the Ballon d'Or 2026 rankings, strengthening his credentials to claim football's most prestigious individual honour. The post Yamal leads Ballon d’Or race after inspiring Spain’s World Cup triumph appear

item.title: Tinubu gave my brother appointment because of Peter Obi – Isaac Fayose
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Isaac Fayose, younger brother to former Ekiti State Governor, Ayodele Fayose, has blasted the new appointment given to his elder brother. In a video posted on his Facebook page, Fayose questioned the rationale behind the appointment. He said president Bola Tinubu quickly gave his brother an appointm

item.title: 2027: US govt now more worried about Muslim-Muslim ticket – Kwankwaso
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Vice-presidential candidate of the National Democratic Coalition, NDC, Rabiu Kwankwaso, has claimed that the United States government is now more concerned about the prospect of another Muslim-Muslim presidential ticket in the 2027 general elections than allegations previously made against him. Kwan

item.title: Uganda: New Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Variant Discovered, Offering Critical Insights for Future Outbreak Response
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nile Post] Researchers from the Interdisciplinary Consortium for Epidemic Research and Response (ICER) and collaborating institutions have identified a previously unreported variant (novel clade) of the Bundibugyo Ebola virus (BDBV) linked to the ongoing 2026 outbreak in Uganda and the Democratic R

item.title: Uganda: Museveni Fires Six Engineers Over Corruption in Road Projects
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nile Post] Tempers flared in Parliament's Committee on Physical Infrastructure after Works and Transport Minister Fred Byamukama revealed that President Museveni had dismissed six engineers from the ministry over alleged corruption in the implementation of major road projects.

item.title: Fuel price: Marketers didn’t suspend petrol loading at Dangote Refinery — IPMAN
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Independent Petroleum Marketers Association of Nigeria, IPMAN, has dismissed claims that its members suspended the lifting of Premium Motor Spirit, PMS, also known as petrol, from Dangote Refinery. The National Publicity Secretary of IPMAN, Chinedu Ukadike, made the clarification in an interview

item.title: Only 10 to 15 percent of judiciary clean – Akinyede
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Lawyer and public affairs commentator, Senator Sola Akinyede, has claimed that only about 10 to 15 per cent of Nigeria’s judiciary remains untainted, lamenting what he described as a deepening rot in the nation’s judicial system. Akinyede made the remark while speaking on Arise Televisio

item.title: Be kind to filmmakers – Bimbo Ademoye tells Nigerians
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: She urged fans to be more respectful and avoid making rude or disrespectful comments towards filmmakers.

item.title: Many Christian voters in the US see Trump as ‘chosen’ by God to lead
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Many white evangelical Christians view Donald Trump as chosen by God to lead despite not seeing him as personally devout

item.title: 2027: Why Atiku needs to step aside – Osita Okechukwu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: An All Progressives Congress, APC, chieftain, Osita Okechukwu, has urged former Vice President Atiku Abubakar to step aside from the 2027 presidential race and embrace the role of a statesman. The APC chieftain is arguing that Atiku’s decision to contest the 2023 presidential election against

item.title: Insecurity: Nigerian Defence Minister, Musa breaks silence on resignation rumour
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria’s Minister of Defence, General Christopher Musa (Rtd), has dismissed claims making the rounds that he plans to step aside amid the persistent insecurity in the country. He disclosed this in a statement on Monday issued by his spokesman, Leah Katung-Babatunde. Recall that repo

item.title: Insecurity: How climate change is fueling conflict, displacing livelihoods across northern Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Sitting beside his cattle on the outskirts of Shanono Local Government Area of Kano State, elderly pastoralist Gidado Isyaku says years of searching for grazing land have become increasingly dangerous. Once, he moved his herd freely across northern Nigeria and into neighbouring Cameroon in search of

item.title: SeedFi expands to Canada, launches Kouriar following FINTRAC, MSB and PSP registrations
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: SeedFi, a financial services company providing credit access and digital financial solutions, has announced the launch of Kouriar, a cross-border remittance platform built to help diaspora communities move money across borders quickly, securely, and transparently. The launch marks SeedFi’s exp

item.title: Minister Umahi and the woman from òkè òhún
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A few years ago, a man was rushed to the family pharmacy. It was late at night. He had contracted the Yoruba’s response to promiscuity, Mágùn, known in the modern parlance as thunderbolt. About four elderly men brought the ‘patient’ in a very bad shape. He was said to have started exhibiting the sym

item.title: Merz Gets a Chance to Reset His Chancellorship After Ally Quits
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The 48-hour firestorm that took down one of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s key parliamentary allies has given him a window to tighten his grip on the policymaking apparatus.

item.title: Codar Accelerates Africa’s AI Talent Pipeline with $1.5 Million Investment
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: As demand for AI and digital skills continues to rise across Africa, Codar has secured a $1.5 million funding round to accelerate its growth and broaden access to technology education.The company, a portfolio venture of KWE4 Africa Venture Studio, has become one of the continent’s fastest-grow

item.title: Fake kits worth £18m seized by police during World Cup
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Counterfeit football shirts worth over £18m were seized in the UK during the course of the World Cup because of safety concerns, with the Fire Service warning the outfits can be a danger to those who wear them.

item.title: Nigeria: Govt Denies Borrowing N80trn, Says Debt Figures Exaggerated
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Leadership] The federal government on Monday dismissed claims that the President Bola Tinubu administration has borrowed about ₦80 trillion in the last three years, describing the figure as exaggerated and a product of accounting adjustments and inaccurate public reporting.

item.title: Palm Slips from Month-High as Weak Crude Dents Biofuel Appeal
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Palm oil fell from a near one-month high following weakness in the crude and vegetable oil complex, denting the appeal of biofuels.

item.description: INTRODUCTION Oil, gas and other mineral resources are ordinarily, natural sources of wealth and well-being of any people when properly harnessed for the common good. Since 1956 or thereabout, when oil was said to have been discovered in Nigeria, the people have paid the supreme sacrifices for this e

item.title: Show HN: Ex Situ – open-source spatial index of displaced cultural artifacts
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Hi, I designed and developed a spatial index that maps museum artefacts as connecting arcs/hyperlinks from their origin site to institutional location/sources. The Index specifically looks at western/euro-american institutions and maps their collection categorised by them under Islamic art, Asian/Af

item.title: NLC pushes for new minimum wage
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) has announced plans to begin a nationwide campaign for a review of the national minimum wage, while pushing for the introduction of a national minimum pension to improve the welfare of retirees. The post NLC pushes for new minimum wage appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NOSCEF urges Tinubu to prioritise security over budget for churches, mosques renovation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Northern Christian Elders’ Forum (NOSCEF) has rejected the reported allocation of more than N8 billion in the proposed 2026 federal budget for the construction, renovation and equipping of churches and mosques, urging President Bola Tinubu to redirect the funds to security, economic recovery and

item.title: Ansaru commanders bag life jail for terrorism
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Following yesterday’s conviction and life-sentencing of the commanders of an Al-Qaeda-linked group, Ansaru, jubilation erupted in so many homes and quarters, especially in the northern part of the country. The post Ansaru commanders bag life jail for terrorism appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Argentina players receive heroes’ welcome home despite World Cup defeat
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Argentina’s players were welcomed home by fans lining the streets as the team travelled from the airport.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Police Command has detained four police officers for allegedly extorting N53,000 from the Chairman of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Dr. Musa Aliyu (SAN), during an illegal stop-and-search operation in Abuja. The po

item.title: Court grants Miyetti Allah leader, Bodejo, N2bn bail in $2.33m money laundering trial
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal High Court, Abuja granted bail to the leader of Miyetti Allah Kautal Hore, Bello Abdullahi Bodejo, in the sum of N2 billion over his ongoing trial for alleged money laundering involving $2.33 million. The post Court grants Miyetti Allah leader, Bodejo, N2bn bail in $2.33m money launder

item.title: 2026 budget: N12.8 trn concealed under service wide vote –Atiku
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Vice President, Atiku Abubakar, has accused the the Federal Government of allegedly concealing N12.8 trillion under the Service Wide Vote in the 2026 Appropriation Act. The post 2026 budget: N12.8 trn concealed under service wide vote –Atiku appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: …Nigeria can’t borrow its way to prosperity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Atiku also said that Nigeria can neither borrow nor tax its way to prosperity, stating that the country’s economic salvation lies in producing what it eats, manufacturing what it uses and exporting what it makes. The post …Nigeria can’t borrow its way to prosperity appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Airport cab drivers’ protest
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE ongoing protest by airport cab drivers against the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria’s (FAAN) directive requiring all airport taxi operators to use vehicles manufactured in 2020 or later deserves careful consideration. While the objective of improving the quality and image of Nigeria’s inter

item.title: Zimbabwe: 2030 No Longer Enough - Zanu-PF Now Pushes to Extend Mnangagwa's Presidency to 2037
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] Barely days after President Emmerson Mnangagwa signed the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act, 2026 into law, ZANU PF Harare Province has begun pushing to extend his tenure beyond 2030 to 2037.

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Eight people have died and nine are still missing after a boat accident in Jigawa State. Search and rescue operations continue for the remaining passengers

item.title: Sudan: U.S. Slaps New Sanctions On Sudan After Chlorine Gas Deployment Claims
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [RFI] The United States has imposed new airspace bans and financing restrictions on Sudan's military-led government, accusing it of using chemical weapons in its war against rival RSF paramilitaries.

item.title: Gabon: France Welcomes Gabon's Oligui As Mineral and Military Ties Defy Wider Rift in Africa
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [RFI] Gabon's President Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema begins a three-day official visit to France on Monday, underlining the close relationship between Paris and one of its few remaining key allies in Francophone Africa.

item.title: Akeredolu at 70: The man who trusted me with his voice, By Bámidélé Adémólá-Olátéjú
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: I make no apology for the loyalty I had for this man, in his life and in his passing. It has become fashionable in Nigerian public discourse to treat loyalty as naivety, and betrayal as sophistication. I reject that inversion. I believe “it is better to be hanged for loyalty than to be rewarded for

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.

item.title: Oyedele: Tinubu Hasn’t Borrowed Up to N80trn, Blames Debt Rise on FX, Legacy Liabilities
